description: Minutes of presbytery meetings have not been approved.
Ordination exam approved without record of internship being completed.
A call approved for labor Out of Bounds gives no reasons for considering it a valid Christian ministry.
Presbytery meeting held without minutes being submitted.
No record of committee members, licentiates or interns in Directory or Roll.
There is no documentation (calls, etc.) for two men for changes from assistant to associate pastor. There are no commissions approved for installation.
response:
Response to 28th GA: These men were already ordained and installed in their particular churches. This was simply a change in their title designation.
Rationale: Change in call from Assistant to Associate requires a new call from the congregation to replace previous call from the session. BCO 20-6, 22-2,3
Response to 29th GA: These two men in question were serving in their churches and still are. Congregational Meetings were called and calls were extended to them as Associate Pastors. There were no changes in the terms of their calls at that time. Calvary Presbytery felt that since these men were already ordained and installed, that a further installation service would not be necessary. If we were wrong in this we will gladly correct the situation, if you will so instruct us. We thought we did everything correctly, but we willingly submit to your wisdom and humbly seek your guidance in this matter if further action is needed.
Rationale why response to 29th GA is unsatisfactory: Change in call from assistant to associate requires a new installation by the Presbytery because the assistant previously called by the session has now been called by the congregation (BCO 22-2,3), and because additional questions must be posed to both the new associate and the congregation at a service of installation (BCO 21-9, 10; footnotes). Therefore, it would be most appropriate for Calvary Presbytery to erect a commission or commissions to install the two men in question as associate ministers.

description: Incomplete record of exam elements.
No record of source of request to dissolve relationship with an Assistant Pastor.
response: This was indeed a serious oversight on our part, and Presbytery has rectified the situation by approving the following motion at our stated meeting October 27, 2001 to dissolve the relationship between TE [name deleted] with Redeemer PCA, Winston Salem, NC as Associate Pastor (minutes of this congregational meeting are attached as Appendix #6). Also, at its October 27, 2001 meeting CCP approved the call of TE [name deleted] as Assistant Pastor of Grace Church, Kemersville. We believe what happened is that all the appropriate transfer steps were not recorded because Presbytery viewed this as an in-house transfer. We will be more careful in the future.

description: No record of Presbytery approving call to TE.
response: This was indeed a serious oversight on our part, and Presbytery has rectified the situation by approving the following motion at our stated meeting October 27, 2001 to dissolve the relationship between TE [name deleted] with Redeemer PCA, Winston Salem, NC as Associate Pastor (minutes of this congregational meeting are attached as Appendix #6). Also, at its October 27, 2001 meeting CCP approved the call of TE [name deleted] as Assistant Pastor of Grace Church, Kemersville. We believe what happened is that all the appropriate transfer steps were not recorded because Presbytery viewed this as an in-house transfer. We will be more careful in the future.

description: Incomplete record of exam elements.

Business conducted at Called Meeting not included in the stated purpose of meeting.

response: I have spoken to the Chairman of the Ministers Committee who is responsible for the action taken concerning the dissolution of pastoral relationship. There was a congregational meeting which was appropriately called and conducted. The Secretary of the committee failed to include the minutes of that meeting in the minutes of the committee meeting. The Chairman of this committee will ensure that from this point on all minutes of Congregational Meetings which dissolve the pastoral relationship will be recorded in their minutes and reported to Presbytery.

description: Teaching Elders were approved to labor out of bounds without following BCO 8-7 and 13-2.
response: We acknowledge that we failed to follow the BCO 8-7 and 13-2 in approving Teaching Elders to labor outside the bounds of Presbytery. Since being made aware of our error we will be careful to follow the proper procedures as outlined in BCO 8-7 and 13-2.

description: No record of congregational meetings to dissolve pastoral relationship.
response: The congregational meeting to concur with Mr. [name deleted]'s resignation was held January 2000 and concurred. The fact was not reported in Presbytery's meeting. Presbytery will seek to be more diligent to include such details in our minutes in the future.

description: Minister examined for transfer with no record of previous ecclesiastical connection.
response: Presbytery failed to request transfer of credentials in a timely manner. The Stated Clerk spoke by phone to the Stated Clerk of the dismissing Presbytery and sent a written request for the transfer. Presbytery failed to enter these in the minutes. Presbytery will seek to be more diligent in the future to do these things in a timely manner and to enter them in the minutes.

description: No record of terms of call. Incomplete record of exam elements.
Call for meeting is not in order. BCO 13-12 requires 3RE's & 3TE's from 3 churches.
response: Great Lakes Presbytery, as well as the former Stated Clerk of GLP, acknowledge their error in listing only 2 Teaching Elders and 2 Ruling Elders representing 3 churches in the call for the meeting, and will be more careful in the future to document the concurrence of at least 3 Teaching Elders and 3 Ruling Elders from 3 churches as required in BCO 13-12.

description: No indication that candidate for licensure preached a sermon as part of his exam.
response: Great Lakes Presbytery verifies that the candidate did, indeed preach a sermon at that meeting and that the sermon was approved by Presbytery. We regret that the itemizing of the individual requirements for licensure was not consistent with the action of Presbytery recorded as 'On separate motions all areas of his exam were sustained', and will be more careful to be consistent in the future.

description: Presbytery approved an exception to WCF 24-3 in which the candidate stated, "the WCF has gone too far in the section on marriage where in Chapter 24-3, it lumps Roman Catholics, infidels, and other idolaters together," allowing him to "express his views" without clarifying what "express his views" means and whether he may practice his views.

Presbytery approved two candidates with exceptions to WCF 24-3 in which they stated, "the WCF has gone too far in the section on marriage where in Chapter 24-3, it lumps Roman Catholics, infidels, and other idolaters together."

As is evident from the minutes, the clerk intended to insert them, but forgot to do so. We have attempted to locate these reports for your review, but have only been able to find one: the Report of the Commission to Install [name deleted] on August 20, 2000. We will submit this report for review by the 30th General Assembly CRPR. The other reports were purged with the assumption that they had been included in the minutes. We will be more careful in this area in the future.

Man approved for ordination who takes the following exceptions (among several others) to certain language in the Confession of Faith: does not agree that "vows of poverty, obedience, and singleness are necessarily sinful and superstitious" WCF XXII. 7; takes exception to the language that "none who are not then present in the congregation" should partake of the Lord's Supper WCF XXIX.3; takes exception to the language of "the making any representation of God, of all or any of the three persons, either inwardly in our mind or outwardly in any kind of image or likeness of any creature whatsoever" WLC #109; takes exception to the language "Although all are not to be permitted to read the word publicly to the congregation..." WLC #156.

response: Presbytery regrets not having approved and appended the minutes of the Commissions to install TE [name deleted] and TE [name deleted] in their November 10, 2000 meeting minutes. The report of the Commission for TE [name deleted] was approved and appended in our January 20, 2001 meeting minutes and likewise the Commission to install TE [name deleted] in our March 9, 2001 meeting minutes. The records for the Commission to install TE [name deleted] seem to have been lost. He was however (re)installed on November 4, 2001 and that Commission report was approved and appended in our November 9, 2001 meeting minutes.

description: A TE, transferring from another denomination was not examined as required by BCO 21-4, nor were there extraordinary provisions invoked, nor was he asked the ordination questions.
response: Presbytery now understand its BCO 13-6 requirement to examine ministers transferring from another denomination in both views and knowledge in all areas of BCO 21-4 and has started that process with its January 12, 2002 meeting when it examined a minister coming from the KAPC. Presbytery also understands its requirement to orally ask the ordination questions of BCO 21-5 at the examination. We also note that we accomplished this in written form when the transferring minister signed the Ministerial Obligation Form. Presbytery notes that our minutes of June 8, 2001 (the examination of Rev. [name deleted] for transfer from the Free Methodist Church) will fail to conform to the above standards and requests the 2002 Committee on Review of Presbytery Minutes to note our current resolve to meet our BCO requirements. (This June 8, 2001 examination was conducted before the 2001 Committee drew our attention to this expanded requirement)

description: Approval of the examination for ordination of a candidate who states as part of his exceptions to the WCF, WLC, and WSC that "I also believe the Sabbath rest to mean any one day in seven."
response: In the examination of Candidate [name deleted] for ordination, Presbytery regrets allowing the candidate's own statement "I believe the Sabbath rest to mean any one day in seven," to appear in our minutes with all its ambiguity. We understand how that statement might be interpreted as the view that we might choose another day of the week besides Sunday as the day for corporate worship. The Committee's references to WCF 21-7 and to WLC 116 imply that they so interpreted [name deleted]'s statement. But that is not his view. His intention is to affirm that ministers, unable to rest on the Lord's Day due to their official duties, are to choose another day of the week on which to rest. Presbytery does not consider this view an unacceptable exception to our Standards and trusts the Committee agrees.

response:
Response to 28th and 29th GA: See 29th GA Minutes 29.64.32
Lationale: The Presbytery has submitted word-for-word the same response submitted to the 29th GA and the same response in substance was submitted to the 27th and 28th General Assemblies. Therefore, the 30th GA believes that the Presbytery still needs to submit a substantive response to the action of these previous General Assemblies.

response: Presbytery regrets the failure to record meeting the provision of BCO 18-2 for Candidates coming under care, and will endeavor to be more inclusive in the future.

description: No evidence of congregational concurrence in dissolution of calls.
response: Presbytery regrets the failure to record the congregational concurrence to the dissolution of pastoral relationship in items 1 and 3. The congregational concurrence was received in each case but not recorded.

description: Two commissions are established with only one ruling elder member.
response: Presbytery regrets the failure to record the proper number of Ruling Elders appointed to commissions. In each case the commissions were compliant with the BCO but the names were not recorded. Presbytery will take measures to ensure proper recording in the future.

description: A candidate is brought under care when he is not present so he could not answer the question or receive a charge. Also, there is no record of a charge or prayer for a candidate who is present.
response: Presbytery regrets the failure to properly record all of the details concerning examination and charge. In the future all candidates will be present and all elements will be recorded, as required.

description: A candidate is approved for ordination with no record in the minutes of a call or of provisions for ordination.
response: Presbytery regrets the failure to include the candidate's call and commission and will endeavor to be more careful in the future.

description: There is no reason given or documentation for removal of a candidate from Under Care Roll.
response: The two candidates were removed because both were ordained.

description: Divesting of TE should report 2nd reading. Also should assign to church membership.
response: The action taken was the second reading (see previous meeting Minutes of the 60th SM of NTP, Page 5, ppg 2101). A letter was received 6 months earlier from the TE wherein he requested divestiture. It was reported to the Presbytery and he was divested at the next meeting. This was not unilateral but based on a request. Our action was in accordance with BCO 38-2. Regarding the exception to BCO 46-8, the TE requesting divestiture had moved to an adjoining state and a town without ap ca church. The TE indicated that he would be attending a different denomination.

description: Examination of TE for transfer from another denomination: He was not examined in Bible content, church history or history of the PCA.
response: The candidate was examined in the previously mentioned fields but the SC failed to record same. The transferring TE was formerly the Moderator of the National Presbyterian Church of Mexico and was examined thoroughly in Committee. However, the point is moot since the TE has been transferred back to Mexico and is no longer a member of NTP or the PCA. In the future, the SC will attempt to record examination action more thoroughly and completely.

description: No record that man was a member of a church of NTP for 6 months or that he had the endorsement of his session.
response: The Stated Clerk inadvertently omitted this information. Letters and records in the files indicate the man was a member of a NTP church for 6 months and the files do contain a letter of sessional endorsement (dated 6/11/2000). The SC will take greater pains not to leave this detail out of the Minutes in the future.

response: PNI has since re-examined the man. His licensure exam was sustained by the presbytery.

Paragraph 31.10, concerning the ordination examination of a man. There is no indication of completion of internship. There is no indication of approval of educational requirements and original languages. There was no sermon approved and no documentation of his call. As he was coming as a Licentiate, there was no indication of his being asked if he changed his previous views concerning the WCF, WSC, or the BCO.
response: Southeast Louisiana Presbytery agrees with the exceptions, except for the preaching portion. We will make every attempt to be more careful in reporting all actions taken by Presbytery. All actions cited in the exception were accomplished but not recorded. In reference to a sermon not being preached by one who is licensed, the examination for licensure requires a sermon to be preached and BCO 21-4 par. #2 specifically states that "If the Presbytery previously approved all parts of the licensure examination, it need not re-examine the intern in those areas at this time." Thus, since the license had preached a satisfactory sermon nine months previously and satisfactorily completed all trails for licensure, he was not required to preach again by Presbytery.

description: In view of the candidate's exception to WCF 11:3, as stated below, which strikes at the fundamentals of our system of doctrine, the Presbytery of Southern Florida erred in sustaining his examination for transfer. "With regard to WCF 11:3, I do not believe that the Holy Spirit eternally proceeds from the Father and the Son, but, in the integrity of the Holy Trinity, from the Father alone. I believe that the references such as John 14:16-17, 26, 15:26, 16:7-15, refer to the out-pouring of the Spirit at Pentecost and not to His eternal procession. While it is true, of course, that the Holy Spirit is the Spirit of Christ, he is also referred to as the Spirit of God, and, as far as that goes, in the mystery of the Holy Trinity, Jesus is referred to as the Everlasting Father. Historically, the addition of the filioque was made unilaterally by the Roman Catholic Church centuries after the Council of Nicea and subsequently inherited by Protestantism."
BCO 13-6 requires thorough exam of transfers in all areas. No extraordinary clause stated as rationale.
response: Presbytery has discontinued using D-21, Check List For Reception of a Minister From Another Denomination, which is part of the "Handbook for Presbytery Clerks." Presbytery is now following BCO 13-6 in all such transfers.

description: BCO 13-6 requires thorough exam of transfers in all areas. No extraordinary clause found.
Theological paper approved in which candidate stated, "I believe that God gives extraordinary gifts (miracles, healing, and tongues)."
response:
The Teaching Elder in question is no longer a member of Presbytery and has left the PCA and joined another denomination.
Rationale: RAO 14-10.b requires that the Presbytery respond by stating whether they agree or respectfully disagree with the exception.

description: Incomplete record of exam elements.
Incomplete record of exam elements.
The purpose of the called meeting is not stated with the minutes.
response: The purpose of this Called Meeting was specified in the Call of May 17, 2000. A copy of which is attached [with the minutes turned in this year]. There were only two items of business to be conducted: (1) Approve the Pastoral Call from Covenant Presbyterian Church, Sun City West, AZ, to TE [name deleted], (2) Conduct appropriate examination of [name deleted] as the RUF Representative at the University of Arizona and approve his moving to the field. As you will note from paragraphs 1 and 2 of the June 30, 2000 minutes, these were the only two items discussed and acted on. BCO 13-12 does not say that the purpose of the Called Meeting has to be explicitly restated in the minutes if it has been clearly stated in the Call which in this case it was. The moderator, RE [name deleted], explicitly ruled that we would follow the order as the business items were listed on the docket. This was done as paragraphs 1 & 2 so indicate. It appears, therefore, that the Southwest Presbytery fully complied with BCO 13-12. However, we have retroactively changed out June 30, 2000 minutes to specifically reflect the two items of business mentioned in the May 17, 2000 Call.
